Lepidium meyenii, commonly known as Maca, is a plant-based superfood that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. Maca is known for its potential health benefits, including enhancing fertility, improving hormonal balance, and reducing symptoms of menopause and andropause. The bioactive constituents of Maca include alkaloids, glycosides, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maca include capsules, powders, and teas, which can be used to support hormone balance, fertility, and overall well-being.
